title: LeetCode Solutions
description: Curated LeetCode write-ups with original summaries, pattern recognition, and clean C++ implementations aimed at interview preparation.
statementPolicy: Official LeetCode problems are linked on every page instead of being mirrored here.
positioning: This branch sits between the DSA notebook and the harder contest archives: the emphasis is clarity, reusable patterns, and recruiter-friendly code.

startHere.label: Two Sum
startHere.description: Start with the complement lookup pattern, then move into sorted-pair and sliding-window variants.

learningPath.title: Build the scan baseline
learningPath.description: Start with one-pass array scans, then learn when sorting turns a quadratic search into a pointer walk.

learningPath.title: Add decision patterns
learningPath.description: Learn the two big interview accelerators: monotonic stacks for next-greater style queries and binary search on a monotone answer.

learningPath.title: Move into traversal
learningPath.description: Practice recursive and graph traversal patterns after the pointer-based problems feel routine.

learningPath.title: Finish with optimization
learningPath.description: Dynamic programming becomes much easier once the earlier recognition patterns are stable.

categories.title: Arrays
categories.description: Hash-map lookups, prefix-style scans, and the baseline decisions that show up in almost every interview loop.

categories.title: Two Pointers
categories.description: Sorted-array pairing, duplicate control, and shrinking a search space from both ends.

categories.title: Sliding Window
categories.description: Grow and shrink a window while maintaining an invariant instead of restarting every substring check.

categories.title: Binary Search
categories.description: Classic sorted-array search plus binary search on a monotone feasibility answer.

categories.title: Stack
categories.description: Monotonic structures and deferred answers for next-greater and range-style problems.

categories.title: Heap
categories.description: Top-k selection, scheduling, and priority-based state expansion.

categories.title: Graph
categories.description: Connectivity, traversal, components, and graph state propagation.

categories.title: Tree
categories.description: Recursive structure, subtree information, and ancestor-style reasoning.

categories.title: Dynamic Programming
categories.description: State design, transition choices, and subproblem reuse without overcomplicating the implementation.

categories.title: Backtracking
categories.description: Search trees with pruning, construction order, and duplicate control.

categories.title: Greedy
categories.description: Local decisions that stay globally safe because the invariant is strong enough.

categories.title: Bitmask
categories.description: Subset state, compact flags, and brute-force acceleration through representation.

categories.title: Advanced
categories.description: Design-heavy problems that combine multiple patterns or ask for a stronger data structure.
